name: HEMANGIOENDOTHELIAL SARCOMA
definition: HEMANGIOENDOTHELIOMA = HEMANGIOEPITHELIOMA neoplasm of vascular endothelial cells of intermediate aggressiveness with either benign or locally aggressive vascular tumor of infancy EPITHELIOID HEMANGIOENDOTHELIOMA OF BONE rare endothelial intermediate-grade malignant vascular neoplasm

demographics.Histo: irregular anastomosing vascular channels lined by one / several layers of atypical
demographics.Path: infiltrative growth pattern with predominant Kaposi sarcoma-like content consisting of

dominant_finding: cortical disruption with extension into soft tissues
discriminating_features: cutaneous thickening, stranding of subcutaneous fat, hemorrhage
ill-defined margins ← involvement of multiple tissue planes
destructive changes and remodeling of adjacent bone
cortical disruption with extension into soft tissues

general: cutaneous thickening, stranding of subcutaneous fat, hemorrhage
ill-defined margins ← involvement of multiple tissue planes
destructive changes and remodeling of adjacent bone
MR: signal intensity similar to muscle on T1WI
heterogeneously hyperintense relative to muscle on T2WI
signal voids on GRE ← accumulation of blood products
low to intermediate signal intensity on T1WI
XRAY: eccentric lytic lesion without matrix mineralization
osteolytic aggressively destructive area with indistinct margins (high grade)
well-demarcated margins with scattered bony trabeculae (low grade)
osteoblastic area in vertebrae, contiguous through several vertebrae
CT: cortical disruption with extension into soft tissues

A thorough evaluation requires CT of chest and abdomen + bone scintigraphy +
clinical.Prognosis: variable course; poor prognosis with visceral involvement; local recurrence
